critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: Target encoding as part of the AutoML pipeline is a legitimate advantage.
Why wrong: The technique is legitimate; the application order is the problem. Fitting target encoding before fold creation introduces leakage that inflates CV accuracy and may have biased pipeline selection within the AutoML search.
Evidence: Leakage-free rerun of AutoML search required.

Issue 2
Claim: Using the test set to find the 80% recall threshold is necessary because there is no separate calibration set.
Why wrong: The absence of a separate calibration set does not justify using the test set for threshold selection. The correct approach is to use cross-validation on the training set to identify the expected threshold for 80% recall, then apply it to the test set without adjustment.
Evidence: Threshold should be identified from cross-validation of the training set; test set used only for final evaluation.

Issue 3
Claim: The baseline comparison adequately reflects what the AutoML system adds over the existing approach.
Why wrong: The baseline was not re-run under any updated preprocessing protocol. Whatever contribution target encoding makes to the AutoML advantage cannot be attributed to model architecture vs. feature engineering differences from the leaky pipeline.
Evidence: Re-run both models under matched conditions.
defender_raw: Round 1: On Issue 1 — concede. Target encoding before fold creation is a leakage error regardless of whether the technique is valid. The leakage-free rerun is required. On Issue 2 — concede. Cross-validation of the threshold criterion on training data is the correct approach. The test set should not have been used for threshold selection. On Issue 3 — concede. The baseline must be re-run under matched conditions for a fair comparison.

debate_rounds.critic: Full concession on all three issues. Critique wins. The benchmark is invalid as presented and must be corrected.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 1: Full concession. Implementation should not proceed on the current benchmark.

all_issues_raised: Issue 1: target encoding leakage before fold creation
Issue 2: threshold selected on test set

empirical_test.condition: Fold-specific target encoding; CV-based threshold selection for both systems; evaluation on independent test set under identical protocols
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: AutoML lift at 80% recall falls below 1 point under leakage-free conditions
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: AutoML shows 0.02+ ROC-AUC and 3+ point precision gain under leakage-free matched conditions
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: ROC-AUC preserved but threshold gain disappears
